description: Trigger discrimination: validation-depth selection (cost routing) vs approval routing (change-classification-gate), tier execution (local-ci-mirror-preflight), and full-tier shard architecture (sharded-validation-with-resume).

cases.prompt: Given the files this PR touches, decide whether we run the docs-only fast path, the fast tier, or full validation — and make the unmatched-file behavior fail closed.
cases.reason: Choosing validation DEPTH from the changed file set with fail-closed semantics is the selector — not the approval/scope gate, and not the execution of the chosen checks.

cases.prompt: Classify this task before we start: what change class is it, does it need human approval, and what file scope is the work locked to?
cases.reason: Class → approval path and scope lock is change-classification-gate; the selector shares the class vocabulary but owns only the validation-cost decision.

cases.prompt: The change is full-tier — now actually run everything CI would run, locally, verified against clean main first, before I commit.
cases.reason: Executing the selected tier locally with baseline and failure classification is the preflight; the selector's job ended when the tier was chosen.

cases.prompt: The full tier takes 45 minutes and dies on timeouts — architect it as named shards with persisted status, resume, and one aggregate required check.
cases.reason: The full tier's internal architecture (shards, status file, resume, aggregate gate) is sharded-validation-with-resume; the selector only decides WHEN the full tier applies.

cases.prompt: The team says this change is 'just docs' but it edits AGENTS.md and a lockfile — settle what counts as docs-only in this repo, with the never-docs-only list written down.
cases.reason: The docs-only definition and its never-docs-only boundary are the selector's artifact (validation cost); no approval-path question is being asked.

cases.prompt: What validation does this change need?
cases.reason: A bare 'what validation does this change need' is the depth/cost decision — this selector. change-classification-gate answers what change CLASS it is and its approval path; the validation-tier question routes here even though the two share the class vocabulary.
